title: Addition and subtraction word problems 2
description: Practice solving more challenging word problems with addition and subtraction. Numbers used are 20 or less.

assessment_items.question_content:
The pirates saw $20$ sea creatures. Some had purple spikes, and the other $4$ had giant blue polka dots.

**How many sea creatures had purple spikes?**

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] $ +$ $4 = 20$
hints:
The pirates saw $20$ sea creatures.

 ![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/a65fd0b777f1b3be86ef04e5d455f081.png)

Count the $4$ sea creatures with giant blue polka dots.

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/276c15eda201e1bd498d86b559e01909.png)

There are $\blue{16}$ left. 
$\blue{16}$ sea creatures had purple spikes.


$\blue{16} + 4 = 20$

assessment_items.question_content:
There were some gorillas lifting weights in the gym. Then $3$ more gorillas came to do yoga. Now $17$ gorillas are in the gym.

**How many gorillas were lifting weights? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] $ +$ $3 = 17$
hints:
There are $17$ gorillas at the gym.  

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/4e632165bb38b7e729fca23f71532ea3.png)
Count the $3$ gorillas doing yoga.

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/1912e0df2a65547b0743cdad96ef1567.png)

There are $\blue{14}$ left. 
$\blue{14}$ gorillas were lifting weights.

$\blue{14} + 3 = 17$

assessment_items.question_content:
$12$ aliens landed in our city. Some aliens were on the first spaceship. The remaining $7$ aliens were on the second spaceship.


**How many aliens were on the first spaceship? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] $ +$ $7 = 12$
hints:
Count the $7$ aliens on the second spaceship.

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/311995cf1888ac3935a0e2fd3788d2b5.png)

There are $\blue{5}$ left. 
$\blue{5}$ aliens were on the first spaceship.

assessment_items.question_content:
The great magician pulled some rabbits out of his hat. Then he pulled out $6$ doves. He pulled $19$ animals out of his hat.



**How many rabbits did he pull out of his hat? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] $ +$ $6 = 19$
hints:
Count the $6$ doves.

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/9d40f5440d20c0486fa547c20cbdb740.png)

There are $\blue{13}$ left. 
He pulled out $\blue{13}$ rabbits.

assessment_items.question_content:
Amy put $9$ toppings on her ice cream sundae. She put some cherries and $7$ chocolate candies. 



**How many cherries did Amy put on her sundae? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] $ +$ $7 = 9$
hints:
Count the $7$ chocolate pieces.


![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/aad656a41eb09f70ef6b1a2c4cb3ecde.png)

There are $\blue2$ left. 
She put $\blue2$ cherries on her sundae.

assessment_items.question_content:
We found some shells on the beach this morning. This afternoon, we found $7$ more shells. In total, we found $11$ shells.

**How many shells did we find this morning? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] $ +$ $7 = 11$
hints:
Count the $7$ shells that we found this afternoon. 

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/02998b96b890971786147a4b6cd27868.png)

There are $\blue4$ left. 
We found $\blue4$ shells this morning.

assessment_items.question_content:
There were $10$ people in the swimming pool. When the wind started to blow, some people got out of the pool. There were $6$ people still in the pool.

**How many people got out of the pool? **  

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] people

Maria took $15$ blocks out of the bin. After giving some of the blocks to Emily, she had $9$ blocks left.

**How many blocks did Maria give to Emily? **
 
[[Ōśā input-number 1]] blocks

I counted $18$ butterflies in my garden. Some flew away. Then I counted $12$ still there. 

**How many butterflies flew away? **  

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] butterflies

$14$ teddy bears had a tea party. Some of them left to get their fur cut. $8$ teddy bears stayed at the tea party.

**How many teddy bears left the tea party? **  

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] teddy bears

A T-Rex made $16$ waffles for his friends for breakfast. $5$ were blueberry waffles and the rest were chocolate chip waffles.

**How many chocolate chip waffles did he make? ** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] chocolate chip waffles

At the fair, I collected $15$ balloons. Some of them blew away, and I had $13$ left.

**How many balloons blew away? ** 
 
[[Ōśā input-number 1]] balloons

I blew $20$ bubbles. My brother popped some of them. There were $5$ bubbles left.

**How many bubbles did my brother pop? **  

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] bubbles

$15$ students signed up for sports teams. $6$ signed up for soccer and the rest signed up for Quidditch.

**How many students signed up for Quidditch? ** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] students
hints:
We can solve this problem in two ways:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{6} \text{ students for soccer}\\
+& \underline{\blue{?} \text{ students for Quidditch}}\\
=&\pink{15} \text{ students on sports teams}\end{align}$

Or:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\pink{15} \text{ students on sports teams}\\
-& \underline{\green{6} \text{ students for soccer}}\\
=&\blue{?} \text{ students for Quidditch}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The zoo got $18$ new animals. $3$ of them are lions and the rest are monkeys.

**How many new monkeys did the zoo get? ** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] monkeys
hints:
We can solve this problem in two ways:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{3} \text{ lions}\\
+& \underline{\blue{?} \text{ monkeys}}\\
=&\pink{18} \text{ new animals}\end{align}$

Or:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\pink{18} \text{ new animals}\\
-& \underline{\green{3} \text{ lions}}\\
=&\blue{?} \text{ monkeys}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Ivy wants to see $20$ birds on her hike. So far, she has seen $8$. 

**How many more birds does she need to see? ** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] birds
hints:
We can solve this problem in two ways:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green8 \text{ birds she saw}\\
+&\underline{ \blue{?} \text{ birds left to see}}\\
=&\pink{20} \text{ birds in total}\end{align}$

Or:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\pink{20} \text{ birds in total}\\
-& \underline{\green8 \text{ birds she saw}}\\
=&\blue{?} \text{ birds left to see}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The ballerina did $13$ spins. She spun to the right $8$ times and the rest of the spins were to the left. 

**How many spins did she do to the left?** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] spins
hints:
We can solve this problem in two ways:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{8} \text{ to the right}\\
+& \underline{\blue{?} \text{ to the left}}\\
=&\pink{13} \text{ total spins}\end{align}$

Or:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\pink{13} \text{ total spins}\\
-& \underline{\green{8} \text{ to the right}}\\
=&\blue{?} \text{ to the left}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
I invited $14$ people to my birthday party. $7$ were boys and the rest were girls.

**How many girls did I invite? ** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] girls
hints:
We can solve this problem in two ways:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{7} \text{ boys}\\
+& \underline{\blue{?} \text{ girls}}\\
=&\pink{14} \text{ people invited}\end{align}$

Or:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\pink{14} \text{ people invited}\\
-& \underline{\green{7} \text{ boys}}\\
=&\blue{?} \text{ girls}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
I have $19$ pets. $17$ of them are fish and the rest are cats.

**How many cats do I have? ** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] cats
hints:
We can solve this problem in two ways:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{17} \text{ fish}\\
+& \underline{\blue{?} \text{ cats}}\\
=&\pink{19} \text{ pets in total}\end{align}$

Or:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\pink{19} \text{ pets in total}\\
-& \underline{\green{17} \text{ fish}}\\
=&\blue{?} \text{ cats}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $20$ ants eating some crumbs. $1$ was a red ant and the rest were black ants.

**How many were black ants? ** 

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] black ants
hints:
We can solve this problem in two ways:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green1 \text{ red ant}\\
+&\underline{ \blue{?} \text{ black ants}}\\
=&\pink{20} \text{ ants in total}\end{align}$

Or:

$\qquad \begin{align}&\pink{20} \text{ ants in total}\\
-&\underline{ \green1 \text{ red ant}}\\
=&\blue{?} \text{ black ants}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$8$ friends went on a camping trip. $5$ of them slept outside in sleeping bags and the rest slept in tents. 

**Complete the number sentences to find how many friends slept in tents.**

$8$ friends in all $- \,5$ slept outside $= $ [[Ōśā input-number 2]] slept in tents

$5$ slept outside $ + $  [[Ōśā input-number 1]] slept in tents $ = 8$ friends in all
hints: $8$ can be separated into two parts:  $\red{5}$ and $\green?$

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: $8 - \red{5} = \green3$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\red{5}$ to get $8$: $\red{5} + \green3 = 8$

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/81d7ff6a45239668f3d41e708c831c6c.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Luca made $9$ sandwiches for the family picnic. $6$ of them were tuna and the rest were cheese.

**Complete the number sentences to find how many cheese sandwiches Luca made.**

$9$ sandwiches in total $- \,6$ tuna $= $ [[Ōśā input-number 2]] cheese

$6$ tuna $ + $  [[Ōśā input-number 1]] cheese $ = 9$ sandwiches in total
hints: $9$ can be separated into two parts:  $\red{6}$ and $\green?$

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: $9 - \red{6} = \green3$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\red{6}$ to get $9$: $\red{6} + \green3 = 9$

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/a87c9bbca530d0941e57de8541233326.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
There were $20$ cars in the parking lot. $11$ of the cars were red, and the rest were green. 



**Complete the number sentences to find how many cars were green.**

$20$ cars total $- \,11$ red cars $= $ [[Ōśā input-number 2]] green cars

$11$ red cars $ + $  [[Ōśā input-number 1]] green cars $ = 20$ cars total
hints: $20$ can be separated into two parts:  $\red{11}$ and $\green?$

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: $20 - \red{11} = \green9$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\red{11}$ to get $20$: $\red{11} + \green9 = 20$

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/849587ba7be304f81e8aeef7856337a6.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $5$ birds sitting on a tree branch. $2$ are robins and the rest are blue jays. 



**Complete the number sentences to find how many of the birds are blue jays.**

$5$ birds in all $- \,2$ robins $= $ [[Ōśā input-number 2]] blue jays

$2$ robins $ + $  [[Ōśā input-number 1]] blue jays $ = 5$ birds in all
hints: $5$ can be separated into two parts:  $\red{2}$ and $\green?$

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: $5 - \red{2} = \green3$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\red{2}$ to get $5$: $\red{2} + \green3 = 5$

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/c27b88fecaf2a88aa75d74217017330c.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Julia baked $12$ pies for the school bake sale. $10$ were apple pies and the rest were blueberry pies.


**Complete the number sentences to find how many blueberry pies Julia baked.**

$12$ pies total $-\, 10$ apple pies $= $ [[Ōśā input-number 2]] blueberry pies

$10$ apple pies $ + $  [[Ōśā input-number 1]] blueberry pies $ = 12$ pies total
hints: $12$ can be separated into two parts:  $\red{10}$ and $\green?$

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: $12 - \red{10} = \green2$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\red{10}$ to get $12$: $\red{10} + \green2 = 12$

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/aa711978cf299f680da7f649ccca4a75.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
I made a necklace for my mother's birthday with $6$ beads. $4$ of the beads were red and the rest were green.

**Complete the number sentences to find how many green beads I used.**


$6$ beads in all $- \,4$ red beads $= $ [[Ōśā input-number 2]] green beads

$4$ red beads $ + $  [[Ōśā input-number 1]] green beads $ = 6$ beads in all
hints: $6$ can be separated into two parts:  $\red{4}$ and $\green?$

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: $6 - \red{4} = \green2$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\red{4}$ to get $6$: $\red{4} + \green2 = 6$

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/68894f8c5f5d06e40d98a3795cc61005.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $10$ wizards living in the magic castle. $5$ of the wizards wear capes and the rest do not.


**Complete the number sentences to find how many wizards do not wear capes.**

$10$ wizards in total $ - \,5$ with capes $= $ [[Ōśā input-number 2]] without capes

$5$ with capes $ + $  [[Ōśā input-number 1]] without capes $ = 10$ wizards in total
hints: $10$ can be separated into two parts:  $\red{5}$ and $\green?$

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: $10 - \red{5} = \green5$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\red{5}$ to get $10$: $\red{5} + \green5 = 10$

![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/5db84fe5a479fc7d26270e68480b916d.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
I bought some cheese pizzas for the party. Then, I bought  $3$ vegetable pizzas. I bought $10$ pizzas for the party.

 **How many cheese pizzas did I buy? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] cheese pizzas

Some squirrels were sitting on a branch. $6$ more squirrels joined them. Now, there are $11$ squirrels on the branch.

 **How many squirrels were on the branch at first? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] squirrels
hints:
![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/e18791084b821319c738aab701b17fce.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{?} \text{ squirrels}\\
+&\underline{ \blue{6} \text{ squirrels joined them}}\\
=&\pink{11} \text{ squirrels on the branch}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Danny bought $18$ presents. He gave some to his family and the other $8$ to his friends.

 **How many presents did Danny give his family? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] presents
hints:
![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/b1ec473b6fdb45093a292fcb77cfcaf9.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{?} \text{ family presents}\\
+&\underline{ \blue{8} \text{ friend presents }}\\
=&\pink{18} \text{ presents in total}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Kira saw some spaceships in the sky. Then, she saw $6$ more. In total, she saw $12$ spaceships.

 **How many spaceships did Kira see first? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] spaceships
hints:
![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/1ac979819209d86966a21c44de5b4ba6.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{?} \text{ spaceships at first}\\
+& \underline{\blue{6} \text{ more spaceships }}\\
=&\pink{12} \text{ spaceships in total}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Some gorillas came on time to school this morning. $10$ gorillas came late. There were $20$ gorillas in school today.

 **How many gorillas came on time? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] gorillas
hints:
![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/44389cabb923fdd1f07586cfc19722f3.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{?} \text{ gorillas on time}\\
+&\underline{ \blue{10} \text{ gorillas came late}}\\
=&\pink{20} \text{ gorillas in school}\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Lisa went apple picking. She picked some apples on Saturday and $4$ more on Sunday. She picked $15$ apples in total.

 **How many apples did Lisa pick on Saturday? **

[[Ōśā input-number 1]] apples
hints:
![](${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/31b813602095936aa1715e2d59f8233b.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{?} \text{ apples on Saturday}\\
+&\underline{ \blue{4} \text{ apples on Sunday }}\\
=&\pink{15} \text{ apples in total}\end{align}$
